reviews.review_msg: Becky was a very kind and caring professor! She explained the material very well and expressed a genuine desire for each student to do well. She provided us with many practice problems to work on every week to practice what we learned in lecture which helped immensely in exams. I would definitely recommend taking this course with Becky.

Genuinely wants you to succeed. This class was a retake for me to better my grade so I was extremely nervous to take it again, but she made it so easy and enjoyable to learn stats. Always very understanding and sweet, just talk to her! Would recommend to take her, our final average wasn't the best so she adjusted our letter grades.

Becky is one of the best profs I've had! I came into the course expecting to struggle, but Becky taught clearly, gave us lots of practice problems, and gave reasonable exams. She has a strong accent and speaks very quickly, but once I got used to her lectures I had no issues at all! She clearly wants her students to succeed. Highly recommended!

Becky is an amazing professor. You can tell she is really rooting for her students success! I usually hate math type classes and had put this class off for 3 years after starting my degree. I finally took it this semester as I need it for a requirement. I ended up loving statistics thanks to Becky, which I am absolutely shocked about.

Great prof! Her accent can be a bit hard to understand sometimes in lectures, but it's not as bad as people make it to be. Just do all the practice questions and tutorial questions and you'll be set for the midterm and final. Genuinely cares for the students in her class unlike a lot of other professors!

BEST PROF at sfu for me. she genuinely wants you to learn and is very approachable. Just attend her lectures and go through the practice qs and lecture slides. she makes the course easy and interesting. HIGHLY RECOMMEND taking a class with her.

The midterm and final were meant to be easy and were actually very hard (especially the final). The midterm was from chapter 1-15, the final was cumulative. I would recommend doing your own learning for this class as the lectures are content heavy and fast. Stay on track with weekly lectures and do all the practice questions that she posts.
her exams were very easy if you understood the basic concepts, do all her practice and you will be good, she doesn't test in depth but know CLT for midterm.
amazing prof, she's very kind and nice. Other than that you have to attend lectures and study in order get a good grade but its fair.

such a fair class she even gave bonus marks as a halloween treat. she really wants u to suceed. just make sure to attend class and know how to do everything she teaches. the midterms and finals are based on the practice MT and extra practice problems

Dr. Becky Lin is one of the most caring instructors I have met in MATH/STAT classes. She wants us to understand the concepts well through assignments, quizzes, and exams. These include endless practice problems and giving assignments in advance. She can also delay assignment due dates if a legitimate reason is given earlier.

She was the best prof I have ever had so far. Watch all the modules properly and you will be fine. If you want the best mark (A+), simply do the extra practice questions she posts for each unit and the midterm and final will be LITERALLY similar to those. She also gives so many assignments and online quizzes to help you boost your mark!!!
Makes the difficult content easy to understand with her explanations. Literally tells us what will be on the tests, and test/assignment/quiz questions are identical to practice problems.

Professor Lin is a good stats prof, like i suck at math and i passed. My one advise would be to study the modules!! Do not leave them for the last minute cuz it will be to difficult to cover all of them a week before your exams and go to her office hours for help. Professor Lin does a lot to help you, she genuinely wants you to succeed.

Becky is a supportive professor who genuinely wants her students to succeed. The course is challenging, but she provides lots of practice problems and sample exams that closely reflect the material. The only drawback was that the final topics felt rushed.

She is a really good prof, gives extra credit. For me, this class was a good GPA booster. If you are taking it online, pace yourself and don't fall behind on the lectures videos. Midterm was fairly easy (average was 73) and final was also fair game if you study (78 average). 
Clear grading criteria
Becky is amazing! Everyone should try and get her as a prof for any stats class she teaches! She is the best!

Becky was very clear with her examinations and was very accommodating. She genuinely cares about her students and wishes the to do well. Do all her practice and if you need help she is there and responds fast to emails. Take stats with her and you'll be set

Take this course! I'm not good at math or stats and I found this very straightforward. Becky does everything she can to support students, offering extra bonus marks and even telling us specifically what will be on exams. If you attend lectures she says "know this for the exam". Overall very great prof and would definitely take another course

Becky is an excellent teacher and really cares about her students. Her lectures are to the point and they always have extra information so its worth attending. Her exams are just like her practice so be sure to do those. The exams are very fairly graded and often curved. Overall a super enjoyable course as long as you do the work provided

She's amazing. I'm not good at statistics and I did well in her class. She has a way of explaining really hard concepts in a way that could make a toddler understand. She also allows written cheat-sheets on midterm day and final day. Bless you, Becky

Taking stats online, and the first half before the midterm is easy enough to understand with her accent. However, after the midterm the content begins to get much harder and half the stuff she says in her lecture videos is literally WRONG and is writing something completely different than what she's saying which often becomes REAAALLY confusing.

Her accent is very thick, which is not a problem except i rlly need to understand what she's saying and I cant. Im not the best at math so taking this with her is very hard because i dont even know what she's talking about. She does provide practice questions tho
love her. take her class. lots of practice questions and just such a sweet prof
great lecture slides and a lot of practice questions that help preparing for exams.

Becky is genuinely sweet and such a chill prof, literally my saving grace this semester - and it's my first one. Although the accent is a bit hard to understand, which is coming from someone of the same culture, there are recordings of the lectures going through the slides, and they're just definitely easy enough to understand!
Going to lectures is a waste of time. She has been changing her grading system for the past semesters. Do the practice problems for each chapter.
Many opportunities to get extra credit and uses questions straight from the practice exams (like 30%). You can write anything on the cheat sheet too. She's nice.

I'm gonna be fr- I did NOT deserve my B- I should've failed the course because I was so dumb and couldn't understand the course material (not math smart at all) but Becky always encouraged me and helped me as much as she could by explaining the concepts to me, and even giving out extra credit to people who simply attended lecture. TAKE HER COURSE!!

This class helped me get over my fear of quantitative courses, Professor Lin made everything so easy to understand. She broke down the logic behind the math so you have a better understanding why things happen/work the way they do, rather than just memorizing formulas or instructions. Her lectures were admittedly hard to get through, but worth it
I know there are comments about her accent, but it's still understandable. She scaled the finals by a lot too. She genuinely wants her students to get good grades, will take a course with her again.

Professor Lin always has the way of making complicated concepts easy to understand. Her classes are well-structured and clearly organized. The exam material is always reasonable, and as long as you carefully complete her practice questions, achieving a high grade is attainable. Overall, she is an excellent and responsible professor.

Becky's teaching is an organizational mess. Inconsistent, unexplained notation and concepts are weirdly generalized, if at all.
 
I think she tries to simplify harder content, which is great, but doesn't do so effectively. The content is easier compared to most, but it could be so much better if she focused on WHY things happen, not just results.

Dr. Lin is one of the best profs at SFU. Statistics is not an easy subject, but her slides and explanations make it digestible and easy to follow. If you do all the practice problems and homework and review the lectures, you are golden for the midterm and final. I attended a few of her online office hours and she is so caring, and super helpful!

If you need need to take stat203 as a required course, please take it with Becky! I suck at math and was scared to take this course after dropping it with swartz (terrible prof), but Becky breaks everything down and the TAs, especially Roxanne are so helpful. Be ready to study hard and consistently go to TA office hours with follow up Qs though.

Amazing professor, she clearly cares about her students a lot. She gives out lots of practice questions and makes sure you understand all of it. The exams were very fair, as some of the questions had the same format but worded differently. Would definitely take her classes again!

She clearly cares about her students and wants them to succeed. Her content reflects on her assignments, exams and quizzes, and everything is marked fairly. It's evident she puts in the work to help her students and although the content can be tricky, she is very good at explaining everything. The course itself is tricky but she makes it easier.

Dr. Lin is really caring and understanding towards her students as I once missed a quiz and she let me take it the next day. Her practice questions are very similar to exam questions so they're very fair. She gives opportunities for bonus marks and she posts lecture videos. She also responds to emails fast, usually within 10 min. Best prof ever.

i enjoyed becky's class so much. she was nice caring and even funny. she gave many opportunities for bonus marks. the exams were pretty much exactly like the practice materials. if i need to take another stats class i hope i can take it with becky.

Very caring and awesome professor. Her passion for her work is apparent and she genuinely wants all her students to do their very best. Granted, I was in a tough position when I took this class and ended up with a low grade. This WAS NOT the professors fault! She gives a lot of recourses and cheat sheets for midterms and the final! Very nice :)

Becky is the actual GOAT! She gives a lot of practice problems and are very similar to questions on the exams. Her accent is a little thick but her lecture slides are very self-explanatory. She wants to see her students succeed :)

The best prof I've had in uni so far. Accent is hard to understand, but she provides you with pre-recorded lecture videos and extra practice, which she sometimes uses as questions in the tests - this is enough to do good on the exams. Gives extra credit opportunities for in-person lectures. Allows aid sheets during exams. Very helpful and caring.

Becky is so sweet and a great prof! She made her lectures available online which was very helpful. Her tests were very fair and the practice problem sets she provided were very reflective of the exams. Don't hesitate to ask her questions during the exams as well she was very helpful at clarifying any confusions I had.

Becky is an amazing professor, you can tell she really cares about her students. Though she does have an accent making it difficult to understand at times, but the concepts are easy enough where it is not a massive issue. Make sure to do her practice problems and you will excel! (plus her daughter is also adorable!)

Very professional and helpful, but my low rating is from how the lectures are organized/taught. There's a lot of misuse/inconsistency in notation, and information is taught in a un-abstract/generalized way. Concepts were purposely unconnected (etc. never providing general forms for Std Errors or Conf Intervals), which doesn't help future courses.

Becky is a great professor. Most of her exam questions come from the chapter practice questions. She gives many opportunities for bonus marks. However she does have quite a heavy accent so it may be hard to understand sometimes but she really cares about you

Do her practice problems, all of them, it will ensure a A and you will understand the concepts. She really wants you to succeed. She is very accessible by emails. Collect the bonus marks. Amazing prof.

One of the best profs I've had at SFU. She really cares and wants you to do well. Most midterm questions are straight from her practice sets, so if you review the lecture notes and do all the practice questions, you'll be fine. She also grades pretty easily on assignments and tests

Most caring prof out there. Truly wants students to succeed! She provides so many opportunities for help, gives lots of practice problems, previous midterms, a cheat sheet, etc.That being said, the content itself was hard (I'm not a math head). Took a while to grasp-- she has a heavy accent making her difficult to understand sometimes.

I don't usually bother to write reviews, but I had to for Becky since she's genuinely one of the best profs I've ever had. She cares so much for her students and that is reflected in how much effort she puts into teaching. She has clear expectations for exams and choosing in-person vs online helped tremendously. Thanks for everything, Becky!

As a newcomer to stats, Dr. Lin made the material as easy to understand as possible, with multiple opportunities for bonus marks based on participation. Quizzes online were open book, assignments were uncomplicated, and support for exams was plentiful. Super well organized! I would recommend for anyone needing a stats requirement!!
Becky is the best prof you will ever have. she is very caring and actually wants you to do well.

This is the best prof I've ever had. Becky is so sweet and cares about her class and students so much. She puts so much effort into everything and wants everyone to do well. Would 100% recomend taking stats with becky.

Becky is AMAZING. she is so nice, caring and accessible outside of class. Her grading and assignments are all clear and definitely graded fairly. You do need to put effort in but if you watch the lectures, read her announcements, and do the practice problems you will do great. The class is super resourceful, and you can do everything online too.

Dr Lin is a great professor. She allowed us to take a bonus quiz and earn an extra 1% just by opening a crowdmark account. Her accent may be tough to follow but her exams are easy. Also, this class has no homework, but 3 quizzes, 2 midterms and a final. Overall great class, would take again with Dr Lin.

Retook this course after failing 203 with Swartz and I feel sane again!! Becky really cares about her students succeeding in her course and explains concepts clearly. She makes a lot of errors in practice/exam questions which was a little confusing to figure out, but she's the sweetest person ever so it's okay
She is the best of all time for Stat 201. You should definitely take it with her !
Her STAT100 is very easy. 10/10 would recommend for B-Sci.
LOVE HER. becky cares for her students and makes the class super easy to follow and provides recorded lectures. she is also very easy going on marking and exams are exactly like practice material.
BEST PROF IVE EVER HAD AT SFU
